---
title: "Cameron & Trivedi -- Ch. 14: Binary outcome models (via StatEcon)"
Authors:
- Colin Cameron
- Pravin Trivedi
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-expand: true
    toc-title: Contents
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
execute:
  enabled: true
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

# Julia setup

```{julia}
using StatEcon
using DataFrames, ReadStatTables, CSV
using Statistics, StatsBase, LinearAlgebra, Printf, Random
using FixedEffectModels, StatsModels, GLM
using FixedEffectModels: Vcov
using CairoMakie
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "png")
using Optim, ForwardDiff
import Distributions
using Distributions: cdf, pdf, quantile, Normal, TDist, FDist, Chisq

_rawval(x) = hasproperty(x, :value) ? x.value : x  # unwrap ReadStatTables.LabeledValue
```

The binary-outcome estimators used in this chapter now live in `StatEcon`
and are called directly below:

  - `stata_logit` — canonical `logit` (Binomial GLM, LogitLink); ch17/ch18
    reuse it.
  - `stata_probit`, `stata_summarize`, `stata_test`, `stata_lrtest`,
    `stata_regress`, `stata_ivregress_2sls` — shared commands from earlier
    chapters.
  - `stata_hetprob` — heteroskedastic probit (`hetprob`).
  - `stata_ivprobit`, `stata_ivprobit_twostep` — joint-MLE and Newey (1987)
    two-step IV probit.
  - `stata_estat_classification`, `stata_estat_gof`,
    `stata_estat_overid_2sls` — postestimation (`estat classification`,
    `estat gof`, `estat overid`).

The by-hand marginal-effects / model-comparison demos (`margins_stata`,
`margins_dydx_atmeans_binary_stata`, `margins_dydx_at_binary_stata`,
`margins_dydx_ame_binary_stata`, `mfx_binary_stata`, `ols_specs_stata`,
`estimates_table_stata`) are kept inline below as pedagogical scaffolding.

```{julia}
# ── Pedagogical inline helpers (by-hand demos kept in the example) ──────────

"""
    margins_stata(β, V, X; at=nothing, at_labels=nothing, over=nothing,
                  over_levels=nothing, mean_fn=:exp, vce_label="Robust",
                  level=0.95, name="_cons", expression=...)

Stata-style `margins` with delta-method SE. Predictive margins (sample
average of `predict` over X), adjusted predictions at a single `at` point, or
predictive margins by factor level (`over`). `mean_fn` selects the link:
`:exp` / `:identity` / `:logit` / `:probit`.
"""
function margins_stata(β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix, X::AbstractMatrix;
                       at::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                       at_labels::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                       over::Union{Nothing,Pair}=nothing,
                       over_levels::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                       mean_fn::Symbol=:exp, vce_label::String="Robust",
                       level::Float64=0.95, name::String="_cons",
                       expression::String="Predicted number of events, predict()")
    n_sample, _ = size(X)

    g9 = function(x; w_::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w_)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        lpad(s, w_)
    end
    commafmt(num::Integer) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end
    function _summary_row(margin, se)
        z      = margin / se
        p      = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
        crit   = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
        (margin, se, z, p, margin - crit*se, margin + crit*se)
    end
    function _compute_margin_at_X(Xm)
        if mean_fn == :exp
            μ    = exp.(Xm * β)
            gvec = vec((μ' * Xm) ./ size(Xm, 1))
            margin = Statistics.mean(μ)
        elseif mean_fn == :identity
            μ    = Xm * β
            gvec = vec(Statistics.mean(Xm, dims = 1))
            margin = Statistics.mean(μ)
        elseif mean_fn == :logit
            η    = Xm * β
            μ    = 1 ./ (1 .+ exp.(-η))
            w    = μ .* (1 .- μ)                # ∂Λ/∂η
            gvec = vec((w' * Xm) ./ size(Xm, 1))
            margin = Statistics.mean(μ)
        elseif mean_fn == :probit
            η    = Xm * β
            μ    = Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), η)
            w    = Distributions.pdf.(Distributions.Normal(), η)
            gvec = vec((w' * Xm) ./ size(Xm, 1))
            margin = Statistics.mean(μ)
        else
            error("mean_fn=$mean_fn not supported (use :exp / :identity / :logit / :probit)")
        end
        margin, sqrt(gvec' * V * gvec)
    end

    if over !== nothing
        title = "Predictive margins"
        factor_name, factor_idx = over
        levels_to_use = over_levels === nothing ?
                        sort(unique(X[:, factor_idx])) : collect(over_levels)
        rows = Tuple[]
        for lev in levels_to_use
            Xm = copy(X)
            Xm[:, factor_idx] .= lev
            m, s = _compute_margin_at_X(Xm)
            push!(rows, (lev, _summary_row(m, s)...))
        end
    elseif at === nothing
        title = "Predictive margins"
        m, s  = _compute_margin_at_X(X)
        rows  = [(name, _summary_row(m, s)...)]
    else
        title = "Adjusted predictions"
        c = collect(at)
        length(c) == length(β) ||
            error("at must have length(β)=$(length(β)), got $(length(c))")
        if mean_fn == :exp
            margin = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(c, β))
            gvec   = margin .* c
        elseif mean_fn == :identity
            margin = LinearAlgebra.dot(c, β)
            gvec   = c
        elseif mean_fn == :logit
            η      = LinearAlgebra.dot(c, β)
            Λ      = 1 / (1 + exp(-η))
            margin = Λ
            gvec   = Λ * (1 - Λ) .* c        # ∂Λ/∂β = Λ(1−Λ)·c
        elseif mean_fn == :probit
            η      = LinearAlgebra.dot(c, β)
            margin = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
            ϕ      = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
            gvec   = ϕ .* c                  # ∂Φ/∂β = φ(η)·c
        else
            error("mean_fn=$mean_fn not supported (use :exp / :identity / :logit / :probit)")
        end
        rows = [(name, _summary_row(margin, sqrt(gvec' * V * gvec))...)]
    end

    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-57s%-13s = %5s\n",
                   title, "Number of obs", commafmt(n_sample))
    Printf.@printf("Model VCE: %s\n", vce_label)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("Expression: %s\n", expression)
    if at_labels !== nothing && !isempty(at_labels)
        max_name_w = maximum(length(string(p_.first)) for p_ in at_labels)
        val_strs   = [Printf.@sprintf("%g", float(p_.second)) for p_ in at_labels]
        max_val_w  = maximum(length, val_strs)
        for (i, p_) in enumerate(at_labels)
            prefix = i == 1 ? "At: " : "    "
            Printf.@printf("%s%-*s = %s\n", prefix, max_name_w,
                           string(p_.first), lpad(val_strs[i], max_val_w))
        end
    end
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |            Delta-method")
    println("             |     Margin   std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95% conf. interval]")
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    if over !== nothing
        Printf.@printf("%12s |\n", first(over))   # factor sub-header row
        for (lev, m, s, z, p, lo, hi) in rows
            lev_str = string(lev isa AbstractFloat && lev == floor(lev) ?
                             Int(lev) : lev)
            Printf.@printf("%11s  | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           lev_str, g9(m; w_=10), g9(s; w_=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p),
                           g9(lo; w_=9), g9(hi; w_=10))
        end
    else
        for (lab, m, s, z, p, lo, hi) in rows
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           lab, g9(m; w_=10), g9(s; w_=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p),
                           g9(lo; w_=9), g9(hi; w_=10))
        end
    end
    println("-"^78)
    return rows
end

"""
    margins_dydx_atmeans_binary_stata(β, V, x_eval, var_idx; link=:logit, ...)

Stata-style `margins, dydx(<var>) atmeans` for a binary GLM (logit or
probit). Marginal effect of `var_idx` at the point `x_eval` with
delta-method SE.
"""
function margins_dydx_atmeans_binary_stata(β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix,
                                           x_eval::AbstractVector,
                                           var_idx::Int;
                                           link::Symbol = :logit,
                                           var_name::AbstractString = "x",
                                           depvar::AbstractString = "y",
                                           vce_label::AbstractString = "OIM",
                                           level::Float64 = 0.95,
                                           n_obs::Union{Nothing,Int} = nothing,
                                           at_labels::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector} = nothing,
                                           expression::Union{Nothing,AbstractString} = nothing)
    η = LinearAlgebra.dot(x_eval, β)
    if link == :logit
        Λ  = 1 / (1 + exp(-η))
        me = β[var_idx] * Λ * (1 - Λ)
        J  = β[var_idx] .* Λ .* (1 - Λ) .* (1 - 2*Λ) .* x_eval
        J[var_idx] += Λ * (1 - Λ)
    elseif link == :probit
        ϕ  = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
        me = β[var_idx] * ϕ
        J  = -β[var_idx] .* ϕ .* η .* x_eval
        J[var_idx] += ϕ
    else
        error("link=$link not supported (use :logit or :probit)")
    end
    se = sqrt(LinearAlgebra.dot(J, V * J))
    z  = me / se
    p  = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
    crit  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo = me - crit * se
    ci_hi = me + crit * se

    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        lpad(s, w)
    end
    commafmt(num) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    expr = expression === nothing ? "Pr($depvar), predict()" : expression
    println()
    println("Conditional marginal effects" *
            (n_obs === nothing ? "" :
             "                    Number of obs = " * commafmt(n_obs)))
    println("Model VCE: $vce_label")
    println("Expression: $expr")
    println("dy/dx wrt: $var_name")
    if at_labels !== nothing
        for (i, kv) in enumerate(at_labels)
            prefix = i == 1 ? "At  : " : "      "
            Printf.@printf("%s%-12s = %s\n", prefix, string(kv.first),
                           string(kv.second))
        end
    else
        println("At  : (means)")
    end
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |            Delta-method")
    Printf.@printf("%12s |      dy/dx   std. err.       z    P>|z|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                   "", 100*level)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                   var_name, g9(me; w=10), g9(se; w=9),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p),
                   g9(ci_lo; w=9), g9(ci_hi; w=10))
    println("-"^78)
    return (; me, se, z, p, ci_lo, ci_hi)
end

"""
    margins_dydx_at_binary_stata(β, V, c; specs, link=:logit, ...)

Stata-style `margins, dydx(*) at(...)` (MER / MEM) for a binary GLM. `specs`
is a vector of `(name, kind, col_idx)` with `kind ∈ (:continuous, :factor)`;
factors use the discrete change and continuous vars the analytic derivative,
both with delta-method SEs at the point `c`.
"""
function margins_dydx_at_binary_stata(β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix,
                                      c::AbstractVector;
                                      specs::AbstractVector,
                                      link::Symbol = :logit,
                                      depvar::AbstractString = "y",
                                      vce_label::AbstractString = "OIM",
                                      level::Float64 = 0.95,
                                      n_obs::Union{Nothing,Int} = nothing,
                                      at_labels::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector} = nothing,
                                      noatlegend::Bool = false,
                                      expression::Union{Nothing,AbstractString} = nothing)
    if link == :logit
        μfn(η)   = 1 / (1 + exp(-η))
        μ1fn(η)  = (Λ = μfn(η); Λ * (1 - Λ))
        μ2fn(η)  = (Λ = μfn(η); Λ * (1 - Λ) * (1 - 2*Λ))
    elseif link == :probit
        μfn(η)   = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
        μ1fn(η)  = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
        μ2fn(η)  = -η * Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
    else
        error("link=$link not supported (use :logit or :probit)")
    end

    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    rows = NamedTuple[]
    for (name, kind, idx) in specs
        if kind == :continuous
            η  = LinearAlgebra.dot(c, β)
            μ1 = μ1fn(η);  μ2 = μ2fn(η)
            me = β[idx] * μ1
            J  = β[idx] .* μ2 .* c
            J[idx] += μ1
            row_label = name
        elseif kind == :factor
            c1 = copy(c); c1[idx] = 1.0
            c0 = copy(c); c0[idx] = 0.0
            η1 = LinearAlgebra.dot(c1, β); η0 = LinearAlgebra.dot(c0, β)
            me = μfn(η1) - μfn(η0)
            J  = μ1fn(η1) .* c1 .- μ1fn(η0) .* c0
            row_label = "1." * name
        else
            error("kind=$kind not supported (use :continuous or :factor)")
        end
        se = sqrt(LinearAlgebra.dot(J, V * J))
        z  = me / se
        p  = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
        push!(rows, (name = row_label, me = me, se = se, z = z, p = p,
                     ci_lo = me - crit*se, ci_hi = me + crit*se))
    end

    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        lpad(s, w)
    end
    commafmt(num) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    expr = expression === nothing ? "Pr($depvar), predict()" : expression
    println()
    if n_obs !== nothing
        Printf.@printf("%-57s%-13s = %5s\n",
                       "Conditional marginal effects",
                       "Number of obs", commafmt(n_obs))
    else
        println("Conditional marginal effects")
    end
    println("Model VCE: $vce_label")
    println()
    println("Expression: $expr")
    println("dy/dx wrt:  ", join([r.name for r in rows], " "))
    if !noatlegend && at_labels !== nothing
        for (i, kv) in enumerate(at_labels)
            prefix = i == 1 ? "At  : " : "      "
            Printf.@printf("%s%-12s = %s\n", prefix, string(kv.first),
                           string(kv.second))
        end
    end
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |            Delta-method")
    Printf.@printf("%12s |      dy/dx   std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                   "", 100*level)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for r in rows
        Printf.@printf("%12s |%s%s%9.2f%8.3f%s%s\n",
                       r.name,
                       g9(r.me;    w=11),
                       g9(r.se;    w=11),
                       r.z, r.p,
                       g9(r.ci_lo; w=13),
                       g9(r.ci_hi; w=12))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    if any(s -> s[2] == :factor, specs)
        println("Note: dy/dx for factor levels is the discrete change from the base level.")
    end
    return (; rows, c)
end

"""
    margins_dydx_ame_binary_stata(β, V, X; specs, link=:logit, ...)

Stata-style `margins, dydx(*)` (Average Marginal Effects, AME) for a binary
GLM. Like `margins_dydx_at_binary_stata` but averaged over the sample.
"""
function margins_dydx_ame_binary_stata(β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix,
                                       X::AbstractMatrix;
                                       specs::AbstractVector,
                                       link::Symbol = :logit,
                                       depvar::AbstractString = "y",
                                       vce_label::AbstractString = "OIM",
                                       level::Float64 = 0.95,
                                       expression::Union{Nothing,AbstractString} = nothing)
    if link == :logit
        μfn(η)   = 1 / (1 + exp(-η))
        μ1fn(η)  = (Λ = μfn(η); Λ * (1 - Λ))
        μ2fn(η)  = (Λ = μfn(η); Λ * (1 - Λ) * (1 - 2*Λ))
    elseif link == :probit
        μfn(η)   = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
        μ1fn(η)  = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
        μ2fn(η)  = -η * Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
    else
        error("link=$link not supported (use :logit or :probit)")
    end

    n_, k_ = size(X)
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    rows = NamedTuple[]
    for (name, kind, idx) in specs
        if kind == :continuous
            me  = 0.0
            J   = zeros(k_)
            for i in 1:n_
                xi = X[i, :]
                η  = LinearAlgebra.dot(xi, β)
                μ1 = μ1fn(η);  μ2 = μ2fn(η)
                me += β[idx] * μ1
                J  .+= β[idx] .* μ2 .* xi
                J[idx] += μ1
            end
            me /= n_;  J ./= n_
            row_label = name
        elseif kind == :factor
            me = 0.0
            J  = zeros(k_)
            for i in 1:n_
                xi = X[i, :]
                c1 = copy(xi); c1[idx] = 1.0
                c0 = copy(xi); c0[idx] = 0.0
                η1 = LinearAlgebra.dot(c1, β)
                η0 = LinearAlgebra.dot(c0, β)
                me += μfn(η1) - μfn(η0)
                J  .+= μ1fn(η1) .* c1 .- μ1fn(η0) .* c0
            end
            me /= n_;  J ./= n_
            row_label = "1." * name
        else
            error("kind=$kind not supported (use :continuous or :factor)")
        end
        se = sqrt(LinearAlgebra.dot(J, V * J))
        z  = me / se
        p  = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
        push!(rows, (name = row_label, me = me, se = se, z = z, p = p,
                     ci_lo = me - crit*se, ci_hi = me + crit*se))
    end

    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        lpad(s, w)
    end
    commafmt(num) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    expr = expression === nothing ? "Pr($depvar), predict()" : expression
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-57s%-13s = %5s\n",
                   "Average marginal effects",
                   "Number of obs", commafmt(n_))
    println("Model VCE: $vce_label")
    println()
    println("Expression: $expr")
    println("dy/dx wrt:  ", join([r.name for r in rows], " "))
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |            Delta-method")
    Printf.@printf("%12s |      dy/dx   std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                   "", 100*level)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for r in rows
        Printf.@printf("%12s |%s%s%9.2f%8.3f%s%s\n",
                       r.name,
                       g9(r.me;    w=11),
                       g9(r.se;    w=11),
                       r.z, r.p,
                       g9(r.ci_lo; w=13),
                       g9(r.ci_hi; w=12))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    if any(s -> s[2] == :factor, specs)
        println("Note: dy/dx for factor levels is the discrete change from the base level.")
    end
    return (; rows)
end

"""
    mfx_binary_stata(β, V, X, coefnames; depvar="y", link=:logit,
        at=nothing, level=0.95) -> NamedTuple

Stata's legacy `mfx` (MEM after logit/probit). Auto-detects binary
regressors (values ⊆ {0, 1}) and uses discrete change for them (`*` row
marker); continuous regressors get the analytic derivative, in a single
compact table that includes an `X` column with the at-point value.
"""
function mfx_binary_stata(β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix,
                          X::AbstractMatrix, coefnames::AbstractVector;
                          depvar::AbstractString = "y",
                          link::Symbol = :logit,
                          at::Union{Nothing, AbstractVector} = nothing,
                          level::Float64 = 0.95)
    c = at === nothing ? vec(Statistics.mean(X, dims = 1)) : collect(at)

    if link == :logit
        μfn(η)  = 1 / (1 + exp(-η))
        μ1fn(η) = (l = μfn(η); l * (1 - l))
        μ2fn(η) = (l = μfn(η); l * (1 - l) * (1 - 2*l))
        link_label = "logit"
    elseif link == :probit
        μfn(η)  = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
        μ1fn(η) = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
        μ2fn(η) = -η * Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), η)
        link_label = "probit"
    else
        error("link=$link not supported (use :logit or :probit)")
    end

    is_factor = Bool[]
    for (i, name) in enumerate(coefnames)
        if name == "(Intercept)"
            push!(is_factor, false)
            continue
        end
        col = X[:, i]
        u   = unique(col)
        push!(is_factor, length(u) <= 2 && all(v -> v == 0 || v == 1, u))
    end

    η_at = LinearAlgebra.dot(c, β)
    prob = μfn(η_at)
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)

    rows = NamedTuple[]
    for (i, name) in enumerate(coefnames)
        name == "(Intercept)" && continue
        if is_factor[i]
            c1 = copy(c); c1[i] = 1.0
            c0 = copy(c); c0[i] = 0.0
            η1 = LinearAlgebra.dot(c1, β)
            η0 = LinearAlgebra.dot(c0, β)
            me = μfn(η1) - μfn(η0)
            J  = μ1fn(η1) .* c1 .- μ1fn(η0) .* c0
        else
            μ1 = μ1fn(η_at);  μ2 = μ2fn(η_at)
            me = β[i] * μ1
            J  = β[i] .* μ2 .* c
            J[i] += μ1
        end
        se = sqrt(LinearAlgebra.dot(J, V * J))
        z  = me / se
        p  = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
        push!(rows, (name = name, factor = is_factor[i],
                     me = me, se = se, z = z, p = p,
                     ci_lo = me - crit*se, ci_hi = me + crit*se,
                     X = c[i]))
    end

    g(x; sig::Int = 7) = begin
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        s
    end

    println("Marginal effects after $link_label")
    Printf.@printf("      y  = Pr(%s) (predict)\n", depvar)
    Printf.@printf("         =  %s\n", g(prob; sig = 8))
    println("-"^78)
    println("variable |      dy/dx    Std. err.     z    P>|z|  [    95% C.I.   ]      X")
    println("---------+", "-"^68)
    for r in rows
        label = lpad(r.factor ? r.name * "*" : r.name * " ", 9)
        Printf.@printf("%s|%s%s%8.2f%8.3f%s%s%s\n",
                       label,
                       lpad(g(r.me;    sig = 7), 11),
                       lpad(g(r.se;    sig = 4), 12),
                       r.z, r.p,
                       lpad(g(r.ci_lo; sig = 5), 10),
                       lpad(g(r.ci_hi; sig = 5),  9),
                       lpad(g(r.X;     sig = 6), 10))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    println("(*) dy/dx is for discrete change of dummy variable from 0 to 1")
    return (; rows, prob, c)
end

"""
    ols_specs_stata(df, formula; robust=false) -> NamedTuple

Fit `regress y x1 x2 …[, vce(robust)]` via GLM (Gaussian / Identity) and
return the NamedTuple that `estimates_table_stata` consumes:
`(; β, se, coefnames, n, ll)` in slopes-first / _cons-last order.
"""
function ols_specs_stata(df, formula; robust::Bool = false)
    needed = StatsModels.termvars(formula)
    dfc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed])
    for c in needed
        col = dfc[!, c]
        if eltype(col) <: Union{Missing, Float32} || eltype(col) === Float32
            dfc[!, c] = Float64.(col)
        end
    end
    m  = GLM.glm(formula, dfc, Distributions.Normal(), GLM.IdentityLink())
    β  = GLM.coef(m); cn = GLM.coefnames(m)
    n  = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)); k = length(β)
    ll = GLM.loglikelihood(m)
    se = if robust
        Xm = GLM.modelmatrix(m)
        y  = Float64.(GLM.response(m))
        e  = y .- Xm * β
        Hinv = LinearAlgebra.inv(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(Xm' * Xm))
        V    = Hinv * (Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(e .^ 2) * Xm) * Hinv *
               (n / (n - k))
        sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V))
    else
        sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(GLM.vcov(m)))
    end
    intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
    slope_idx     = setdiff(1:k, [intercept_idx])
    ord = intercept_idx === nothing ? collect(slope_idx) :
                                      vcat(collect(slope_idx), [intercept_idx])
    cn_ord = intercept_idx === nothing ? cn[ord] :
                                          vcat(cn[slope_idx], "_cons")
    return (; β = β[ord], se = se[ord], coefnames = cn_ord, n, ll)
end

"""
    estimates_table_stata(specs; stats=[:N, :ll], max_per_block=5)

Stata-style `estimates table m1 m2 …, t stats(N ll) b(%7.3f) stfmt(%8.2f)`.
Compares multiple fitted models side-by-side, with point estimates on the
first line of each variable's row and the t-statistic (β/SE) on the second.
Each `spec` is `(; name, eq, β, se, coefnames, n, ll)`.
"""
function estimates_table_stata(specs::AbstractVector;
                               stats::AbstractVector{Symbol} = [:N, :ll],
                               max_per_block::Int = 5)
    var_w = 12
    col_w = 11
    nice(v) = v == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : v

    eqvars = Tuple{String,String}[]
    for s in specs, v in s.coefnames
        tup = (s.eq, nice(v))
        tup in eqvars || push!(eqvars, tup)
    end
    eqs = String[]
    for (eq, _) in eqvars
        eq in eqs || push!(eqs, eq)
    end

    fmt_b(x)  = Printf.@sprintf("%11.3f", x)
    fmt_t(x)  = Printf.@sprintf("%11.2f", x)
    fmt_n(x)  = Printf.@sprintf("%11d",   x)
    fmt_ll(x) = Printf.@sprintf("%11.2f", x)
    blank     = " "^col_w

    n_blocks = ceil(Int, length(specs) / max_per_block)
    for b in 1:n_blocks
        ix    = ((b-1)*max_per_block + 1):min(b*max_per_block, length(specs))
        chunk = specs[ix]
        nc    = length(chunk)
        line_w = var_w + 2 + nc * col_w
        println("-" ^ line_w)

        print(lpad("Variable", var_w), " |")
        for c in chunk
            print(lpad(c.name, col_w))
        end
        println()
        println("-" ^ var_w, "+", "-" ^ (line_w - var_w - 1))

        for eq in eqs
            println(rpad(eq, var_w), " |")
            vars_in_eq = [v for (e, v) in eqvars if e == eq]
            for v in vars_in_eq
                print(lpad(v, var_w), " |")
                for c in chunk
                    if c.eq == eq
                        cnames = nice.(c.coefnames)
                        i      = findfirst(==(v), cnames)
                        print(i === nothing ? blank : fmt_b(c.β[i]))
                    else
                        print(blank)
                    end
                end
                println()
                print(" " ^ var_w, " |")
                for c in chunk
                    if c.eq == eq
                        cnames = nice.(c.coefnames)
                        i      = findfirst(==(v), cnames)
                        print(i === nothing ? blank :
                              fmt_t(c.β[i] / c.se[i]))
                    else
                        print(blank)
                    end
                end
                println()
            end
            println("-" ^ var_w, "+", "-" ^ (line_w - var_w - 1))
        end

        println(rpad("Statistics", var_w), " |")
        for st in stats
            print(lpad(string(st), var_w), " |")
            for c in chunk
                if st == :N
                    print(fmt_n(c.n))
                elseif st == :ll
                    print(fmt_ll(c.ll))
                else
                    val = getfield(c, st)
                    print(Printf.@sprintf("%11g", val))
                end
            end
            println()
        end
        println("-" ^ line_w)
        println(lpad("Legend: b/t", line_w))
        println()
    end
    return nothing
end
```

# 14 Binary outcome models

## 14.1 Introduction

## 14.2 Some parametric models

### 14.2.1 Basic model

### 14.2.2 logit, probit, linear probability, and clog-log models

## 14.3 Estimation

### 14.3.1 latent-variable interpretation and identification

### 14.3.2 ML estimation

### 14.3.3 The logit and probit commands

### 14.3.4 Robust estimate of the VCE

### 14.3.5 OLS estimation of LPM

## 14.4 Example

### 14.4.1 Data description

```{julia}
#=
clear
* Load data
use musr/mus14data.dta
* Interaction variables
drop age2 agefem agechr agewhi
* Summary statistics of variables
global xlist age hstatusg hhincome educyear married hisp
generate linc = ln(hhinc)
global extralist linc female white chronic adl sretire
summarize ins retire $xlist $extralist
=#

# Load mus14data, drop the four interaction columns Stata recreates later,
# add linc = log(hhincome), and run `stata_summarize` on the combined
# `xlist + extralist` variable list.
df14 = dataset("mus14data")
for c in DataFrames.names(df14)
    if eltype(df14[!, c]) <: Union{Missing, Float32}
        df14[!, c] = convert.(Union{Missing, Float64}, df14[!, c])
    end
end
DataFrames.select!(df14, DataFrames.Not([:age2, :agefem, :agechr, :agewhi]))
# Stata's `gen linc = log(hhincome)` returns missing for hhincome ≤ 0.
# Mimic that here so GLM.jl's IRLS doesn't get fed non-finite values.
df14.linc = [(ismissing(h) || h <= 0) ? missing : log(Float64(h))
             for h in df14.hhincome]

xlist      = [:age, :hstatusg, :hhincome, :educyear, :married, :hisp]
extralist  = [:linc, :female, :white, :chronic, :adl, :sretire]
stata_summarize(df14, vcat([:ins, :retire], xlist, extralist));
```

### 14.4.2 logit regression

```{julia}
#=
* Logit regression
logit ins retire $xlist
=#

# `stata_logit` fits a Binomial GLM with LogitLink and prints Stata's exact
# `logit` table: "Logistic regression" header with Number of obs / LR chi2(k)
# / Prob > chi2 / Log likelihood / Pseudo R², and the coefficient table in
# slopes-first / _cons-last order.
logit_fit = stata_logit(df14,
    GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome + educyear +
                       married + hisp));
```

### 14.4.3 Comparison of binary models and parameter estimates

```{julia}
#=
* Estimation of several models
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
estimates store blogit
quietly probit ins retire $xlist
estimates store bprobit
quietly regress ins retire $xlist
estimates store bols
quietly logit ins retire $xlist, vce(robust)
estimates store blogitr
quietly probit ins retire $xlist, vce(robust)
estimates store bprobitr
quietly regress ins retire $xlist, vce(robust)
estimates store bolsr

* Table for comparing models
estimates table blogit blogitr bprobit bprobitr bols bolsr, /*
   */ t stats(N ll) b(%7.3f) stfmt(%8.2f)
=#

# Fit the six models via `stata_logit` / `stata_probit` / `ols_specs_stata`
# (inline), then `estimates_table_stata` (inline) renders the Stata
# `estimates table … , t stats(N ll)` side-by-side comparison with point
# estimates on the b-row and t = β/SE on the t-row. Logit/probit coefficients
# appear under the depvar equation block ("ins"), regress under "_" (Stata's
# convention for OLS without an equation prefix).
fml_ins = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                              educyear + married + hisp)

blogit   = stata_logit(df14,  fml_ins;                  quiet = true)
blogitr  = stata_logit(df14,  fml_ins; vce = :robust,   quiet = true)
bprobit  = stata_probit(df14, fml_ins;                  quiet = true)
bprobitr = stata_probit(df14, fml_ins; vce = :robust,   quiet = true)
bols     = ols_specs_stata(df14, fml_ins; robust = false)
bolsr    = ols_specs_stata(df14, fml_ins; robust = true)

specs = [
    (name = "blogit",   eq = "ins", β = blogit.β,   se = blogit.se,
                                    coefnames = blogit.coefnames,
                                    n = blogit.n,   ll = blogit.ll),
    (name = "blogitr",  eq = "ins", β = blogitr.β,  se = blogitr.se,
                                    coefnames = blogitr.coefnames,
                                    n = blogitr.n,  ll = blogitr.ll),
    (name = "bprobit",  eq = "ins", β = bprobit.β,  se = bprobit.se,
                                    coefnames = bprobit.coefnames,
                                    n = bprobit.n,  ll = bprobit.ll),
    (name = "bprobitr", eq = "ins", β = bprobitr.β, se = bprobitr.se,
                                    coefnames = bprobitr.coefnames,
                                    n = bprobitr.n, ll = bprobitr.ll),
    (name = "bols",     eq = "_",   β = bols.β,     se = bols.se,
                                    coefnames = bols.coefnames,
                                    n = bols.n,     ll = bols.ll),
    (name = "bolsr",    eq = "_",   β = bolsr.β,    se = bolsr.se,
                                    coefnames = bolsr.coefnames,
                                    n = bolsr.n,    ll = bolsr.ll),
]
estimates_table_stata(specs);
```

## 14.5 Hypothesis and specification tests

### 14.5.1 Wald tests

```{julia}
#=
* Wald test for zero interactions
generate age2 = age*age
generate agefem = age*female
generate agechr = age*chronic
generate agewhi = age*white
global intlist age2 agefem agechr agewhi
quietly logit ins retire $xlist $intlist
test $intlist
=#

# Add the four interaction columns to df14 (idempotent — overwrites any
# existing values), fit logit quietly, and run the joint Wald test of
# `H0: age2 = agefem = agechr = agewhi = 0` using `stata_test`.
df14.age2    = df14.age .* df14.age
df14.agefem  = df14.age .* df14.female
df14.agechr  = df14.age .* df14.chronic
df14.agewhi  = df14.age .* df14.white

fml_int = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome + educyear +
                              married + hisp +
                              age2 + agefem + agechr + agewhi)
logit_int_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_int; quiet = true)

stata_test(logit_int_fit.model, ["age2", "agefem", "agechr", "agewhi"]);
```

### 14.5.2 likelihood-ratio tests

```{julia}
#=
* Likelihood-ratio test
quietly logit ins retire $xlist $intlist
estimates store B
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
lrtest B
=#

# Fit the unrestricted (with the four interactions) and restricted (xlist
# only) logits quietly, then `stata_lrtest` computes
# LR = 2·(ll_unr − ll_res) ~ χ²(df) and prints Stata's standard `lrtest`
# block. df = 4 (restrictions).
fml_unr = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome + educyear +
                              married + hisp +
                              age2 + agefem + agechr + agewhi)
fml_res = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome + educyear +
                              married + hisp)

logit_unr = stata_logit(df14, fml_unr; quiet = true)
logit_res = stata_logit(df14, fml_res; quiet = true)

stata_lrtest(logit_unr.ll, logit_res.ll, 4);
```

### 14.5.3 Additional model-specification tests

#### Lagrange multiplier test of generalized logit

```{julia}
#=
* Heteroskedastic probit model
hetprob ins retire $xlist, het(chronic) nolog // Heteroskedastic Probit
=#

# `stata_hetprob` fits the heteroskedastic probit
#     y = 1{x'β + ε > 0},  ε ~ N(0, σ²),  σ = exp(z'γ)
# jointly in (β, γ) by MLE (LBFGS with an explicit ForwardDiff gradient,
# warm-started from a plain probit), with OIM SEs from the Hessian. Prints
# Stata's exact `hetprob` output: header (Number of obs / Zero / Nonzero
# outcomes / Wald chi2 / Log likelihood / Prob > chi2), the two-block
# coefficient table (`ins` + `lnsigma`), and the LR test of σ-constancy.
stata_hetprob(df14,
    GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome + educyear +
                        married + hisp),
    [:chronic]);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Stukel score or LM test for asymmetric h-family logit
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
predict xbhat, xb
generate xbhatsq = xbhat^2
quietly logit ins retire $xlist xbhatsq
test xbhatsq
=#

# Stukel's LM/score test for the asymmetric h-family logit. Fit the base
# logit, build xbhat (linear index) and xbhatsq, refit with the squared
# index added, and test H0: γ_{xbhatsq} = 0 via `stata_test` — under H0 this
# is the LM/Wald form of Stukel's test.
fml_base = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                              educyear + married + hisp)
base_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_base; quiet = true)

df14.xbhat   = base_fit.X_glm * base_fit.β_glm
df14.xbhatsq = df14.xbhat .^ 2

fml_stukel = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                                 educyear + married + hisp + xbhatsq)
stukel_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_stukel; quiet = true)

stata_test(stukel_fit.model, ["xbhatsq"]);
```

#### Heteroskedastic probit regression

```{julia}
#=
* Heteroskedastic probit model
hetprob ins retire $xlist, het(chronic) nolog // Heteroskedastic Probit
=#

# Same `hetprob` command as the LM-test sub-section above; reproduced here
# under the "Heteroskedastic probit regression" heading. `stata_hetprob`
# handles the full Stata layout.
stata_hetprob(df14,
    GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome + educyear +
                        married + hisp),
    [:chronic]);
```

### 14.5.4 Model comparison

## 14.6 Goodness of fit and prediction

### 14.6.1 Pseudo-R2 measure

### 14.6.2 Comparing predicted probabilities with sample frequencies

```{julia}
#=
* Hosmer-Lemeshow gof test with 4 groups
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
estat gof, group(4)  // Hosmer-Lemeshow gof test
=#

# Fit logit quietly, get fitted probabilities, and call `stata_estat_gof`
# for the Hosmer–Lemeshow GOF block.
fml_gof = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                              educyear + married + hisp)
gof_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_gof; quiet = true)
phat    = GLM.predict(gof_fit.model)
stata_estat_gof(df14.ins, phat, 4; depvar = "ins");
```

```{julia}
#=
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
* Hosmer-Lemeshow gof test with 10 groups
estat gof, group(10)  // Hosmer-Lemeshow gof test
=#

# Same logit fit / fitted probabilities as the 4-group case above; just call
# `stata_estat_gof` with `g = 10`. The header block (`Goodness-of-fit test
# after logistic model` / `Variable: ins`) is emitted because `depvar` is
# provided.
stata_estat_gof(df14.ins, phat, 10; depvar = "ins");
```

### 14.6.3 Comparing predicted outcomes with actual outcomes

```{julia}
#=
* Comparing fitted probability and dichotomous outcome
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
estat classification
=#

# Fit logit quietly, then `stata_estat_classification` prints Stata's full
# classification block: confusion matrix, sensitivity / specificity / PPV /
# NPV, false +/− rates for true ~D / D / classified +/−, and the % correctly
# classified line.
fml_cls = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                              educyear + married + hisp)
cls_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_cls; quiet = true)
stata_estat_classification(cls_fit.model, df14; depvar = "ins");
```

### 14.6.4 The predict command for fitted probabilities

```{julia}
#=
* Calculate and summarize fitted probabilities
quietly logit ins hhincome
predict plogit, pr
quietly probit ins hhincome
predict pprobit, pr
quietly regress ins hhincome
predict pols, xb
summarize ins plogit pprobit pols
=#

# Fit the three single-regressor models quietly (`stata_logit` / `stata_probit`
# for the binary-link models, and `FixedEffectModels.reg` for OLS), get fitted
# probabilities, then summarize via `stata_summarize`.
fml_hh    = GLM.@formula(ins ~ hhincome)
logit_hh  = stata_logit(df14, fml_hh; quiet = true)
probit_hh = stata_probit(df14, fml_hh; quiet = true)
ols_hh    = FixedEffectModels.reg(df14, fml_hh)

df14.plogit  = GLM.predict(logit_hh.model)
df14.pprobit = GLM.predict(probit_hh.model)
df14.pols    = FixedEffectModels.predict(ols_hh, df14)

stata_summarize(df14, [:ins, :plogit, :pprobit, :pols]);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Following gives Figure mus14fig1.eps
sort hhincome
graph twoway (scatter ins hhincome, msize(vsmall) jitter(3)) /*
  */ (line plogit hhincome, clstyle(p1)) /*
  */ (line pprobit hhincome, clstyle(p2)) /*
  */ (line pols hhincome, clstyle(p3)), /*
  */ scale (1.2) plotregion(style(none)) /*
  */ title("Predicted Probabilities Across Models") /*
  */ xtitle("HHINCOME (hhincome)", size(medlarge)) xscale(titlegap(*5)) /*
  */ ytitle("Predicted probability", size(medlarge)) yscale(titlegap(*5)) /*
  */ legend(pos(1) ring(0) col(1)) legend(size(small)) /*
  */ legend(label(1 "Actual Data (jittered)") label(2 "Logit") /*
  */         label(3 "Probit") label(4 "OLS"))
graph export musr/mus14fig1.eps, replace
=#

# Replicates Stata's `graph twoway` twoway-plot of fitted probabilities from
# the three single-regressor models (logit, probit, OLS) overlaid on a
# jittered scatter of the actual binary outcome. Uses `df14.plogit / pprobit /
# pols` produced by the chunk above. Saved as `mus14fig1.png`.
Random.seed!(10101)
sort_idx = sortperm(df14.hhincome)
hhinc_s  = df14.hhincome[sort_idx]

fig_mus14 = CairoMakie.Figure(size = (700, 500))
ax_mus14  = CairoMakie.Axis(fig_mus14[1, 1];
    title  = "Predicted Probabilities Across Models",
    xlabel = "HHINCOME (hhincome)",
    ylabel = "Predicted probability")

CairoMakie.scatter!(ax_mus14, df14.hhincome,
    df14.ins .+ 0.02 .* Random.randn(DataFrames.nrow(df14));
    markersize = 2, color = (:gray, 0.3), label = "Actual Data (jittered)")
CairoMakie.lines!(ax_mus14, hhinc_s, df14.plogit[sort_idx];  label = "Logit")
CairoMakie.lines!(ax_mus14, hhinc_s, df14.pprobit[sort_idx]; label = "Probit")
CairoMakie.lines!(ax_mus14, hhinc_s, df14.pols[sort_idx];    label = "OLS")
CairoMakie.axislegend(ax_mus14; position = :rt)

CairoMakie.save("mus14fig1.png", fig_mus14)
fig_mus14
```

### 14.6.5 The prvalue command for fitted probabilities

```{julia}
#=
* Fitted probabilities for selected baseline using margins (prvalue no longer available)
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
* Calculate predicted probabilities at specific values
margins, at(age=65 retire=0 hstatusg=1 hhincome=50 educyear=17 married=1 hisp=0)
* Or use margins for marginal effects of hhincome
margins, dydx(hhincome) atmeans
=#

# Fit logit quietly. Then run two `margins` calls:
#   (1) Adjusted prediction  Pr(ins=1)  at the user-specified point — uses the
#       inline `margins_stata` with the `mean_fn = :logit` branch.
#   (2) dy/dx of hhincome at means — uses the inline
#       `margins_dydx_atmeans_binary_stata` with `link = :logit`.
fml_mar = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                              educyear + married + hisp)
mar_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_mar; quiet = true)
β_mar   = mar_fit.β_glm
V_mar   = mar_fit.V_glm
cn_mar  = mar_fit.coefnames_glm
X_mar   = mar_fit.X_glm
n_mar   = mar_fit.n

# (1) margins, at(age=65 retire=0 hstatusg=1 hhincome=50 educyear=17 married=1 hisp=0)
at_dict = Dict("(Intercept)" => 1.0,
               "retire"      => 0.0,
               "age"         => 65.0,
               "hstatusg"    => 1.0,
               "hhincome"    => 50.0,
               "educyear"    => 17.0,
               "married"     => 1.0,
               "hisp"        => 0.0)
c_at = [at_dict[name] for name in cn_mar]
margins_stata(β_mar, V_mar, X_mar;
              at = c_at,
              at_labels = [:retire => 0, :age => 65, :hstatusg => 1,
                           :hhincome => 50, :educyear => 17,
                           :married => 1, :hisp => 0],
              mean_fn    = :logit,
              vce_label  = "OIM",
              expression = "Pr(ins), predict()")

# (2) margins, dydx(hhincome) atmeans
x̄ = vec(Statistics.mean(X_mar, dims = 1))
idx_hh = findfirst(==("hhincome"), cn_mar)
margins_dydx_atmeans_binary_stata(β_mar, V_mar, x̄, idx_hh;
    link       = :logit,
    var_name   = "hhincome",
    depvar     = "ins",
    vce_label  = "OIM",
    n_obs      = n_mar,
    at_labels  = [n => Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", x̄[i])
                  for (i, n) in enumerate(cn_mar) if n != "(Intercept)"]);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Comparing fitted probability and dichotomous outcome
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
estat classification
=#

# Same as the §14.6.3 chunk: fit logit quietly, then
# `stata_estat_classification` prints Stata's full classification block.
fml_cls2 = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                               educyear + married + hisp)
cls_fit2 = stata_logit(df14, fml_cls2; quiet = true)
stata_estat_classification(cls_fit2.model, df14; depvar = "ins");
```

## 14.7 Marginal effects

### 14.7.1 Marginal effect at a representative value (MER)

```{julia}
#=
* Marginal effects (MER) after logit
quietly logit ins i.retire age i.hstatusg hhincome educyear i.married i.hisp
margins, dydx(*) at (retire=1 age=75 hstatusg=1 hhincome=35 educyear=12   ///
 married=1 hisp=1) noatlegend   // (MER)
=#

# `margins, dydx(*) at(...)` — MER after logit with `i.<var>` factor
# variables. The inline `margins_dydx_at_binary_stata` handles the mixed
# factor (discrete change) + continuous (analytic derivative) case with
# delta-method SEs and prints Stata's `Conditional marginal effects` block.
# `noatlegend` suppresses the "At:" listing (mirrors Stata).
fml_mer = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                              educyear + married + hisp)
mer_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_mer; quiet = true)
β_mer   = mer_fit.β_glm
V_mer   = mer_fit.V_glm
cn_mer  = mer_fit.coefnames_glm

# Build the at-point c (in β order: intercept first, then formula order).
at_dict = Dict("(Intercept)" => 1.0, "retire" => 1.0, "age" => 75.0,
               "hstatusg" => 1.0, "hhincome" => 35.0, "educyear" => 12.0,
               "married" => 1.0, "hisp" => 1.0)
c_mer = [at_dict[name] for name in cn_mer]

# Specs for each variable: factor (i.<var>) vs continuous.
specs_mer = [
    ("retire",   :factor,     findfirst(==("retire"),   cn_mer)),
    ("age",      :continuous, findfirst(==("age"),      cn_mer)),
    ("hstatusg", :factor,     findfirst(==("hstatusg"), cn_mer)),
    ("hhincome", :continuous, findfirst(==("hhincome"), cn_mer)),
    ("educyear", :continuous, findfirst(==("educyear"), cn_mer)),
    ("married",  :factor,     findfirst(==("married"),  cn_mer)),
    ("hisp",     :factor,     findfirst(==("hisp"),     cn_mer)),
]

margins_dydx_at_binary_stata(β_mer, V_mer, c_mer;
    specs      = specs_mer,
    link       = :logit,
    depvar     = "ins",
    vce_label  = "OIM",
    n_obs      = mer_fit.n,
    noatlegend = true);
```

### 14.7.2 Marginal effect at the mean (MEM)

```{julia}
#=
* Marginal effects (MEM) after logit
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
mfx // (MEM)
=#

# `mfx` — Stata's legacy MEM command. The inline `mfx_binary_stata`
# auto-detects binary regressors (values ⊆ {0, 1}) and uses discrete change
# for them (rows marked `*`); continuous regressors get the analytic
# derivative. Output matches mfx exactly: header (y = Pr(ins) (predict) /
# value), then the variable rows with the `X` column, and the trailing
# `(*) dy/dx is for discrete change ...` footer.
fml_mem = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                              educyear + married + hisp)
mem_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_mem; quiet = true)
mfx_binary_stata(mem_fit.β_glm, mem_fit.V_glm, mem_fit.X_glm,
                 mem_fit.coefnames_glm;
                 depvar = "ins", link = :logit);
```

### 14.7.3 Average marginal effect (AME)

```{julia}
#=
* Marginal effects (MEM) after logit
quietly logit ins i.retire age i.hstatusg hhincome educyear i.married i.hisp
margins, dydx(*) atmean noatlegend  // (MEM)
=#

# `margins, dydx(*) atmean` after logit with `i.<var>` factor variables. Same
# factor/continuous specs as the §14.7.1 (MER) chunk, but evaluated at the
# sample mean point (c = x̄). The inline `margins_dydx_at_binary_stata` prints
# Stata's "Conditional marginal effects" block plus the "Note: dy/dx for
# factor levels ..." footer (since at least one factor is in `specs`).
fml_mem2 = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                               educyear + married + hisp)
mem2_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_mem2; quiet = true)
β_mem2   = mem2_fit.β_glm
V_mem2   = mem2_fit.V_glm
cn_mem2  = mem2_fit.coefnames_glm
X_mem2   = mem2_fit.X_glm

x̄_mem2 = vec(Statistics.mean(X_mem2, dims = 1))

specs_mem2 = [
    ("retire",   :factor,     findfirst(==("retire"),   cn_mem2)),
    ("age",      :continuous, findfirst(==("age"),      cn_mem2)),
    ("hstatusg", :factor,     findfirst(==("hstatusg"), cn_mem2)),
    ("hhincome", :continuous, findfirst(==("hhincome"), cn_mem2)),
    ("educyear", :continuous, findfirst(==("educyear"), cn_mem2)),
    ("married",  :factor,     findfirst(==("married"),  cn_mem2)),
    ("hisp",     :factor,     findfirst(==("hisp"),     cn_mem2)),
]

margins_dydx_at_binary_stata(β_mem2, V_mem2, x̄_mem2;
    specs      = specs_mem2,
    link       = :logit,
    depvar     = "ins",
    vce_label  = "OIM",
    n_obs      = mem2_fit.n,
    noatlegend = true);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Marginal effects (AME) after logit
quietly logit ins i.retire age i.hstatusg hhincome educyear i.married i.hisp
margins, dydx(*) noatlegend        // (AME)
=#

# `margins, dydx(*)` (no `atmeans`) — AME, averaged over the sample. Same
# factor/continuous specs as §14.7.1/§14.7.2, but the inline
# `margins_dydx_ame_binary_stata` loops over the rows of X and averages both
# the ME and its Jacobian. Title becomes "Average marginal effects" and the
# trailing factor-note footer is emitted automatically.
fml_ame = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                              educyear + married + hisp)
ame_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_ame; quiet = true)
β_ame   = ame_fit.β_glm
V_ame   = ame_fit.V_glm
cn_ame  = ame_fit.coefnames_glm
X_ame   = ame_fit.X_glm

specs_ame = [
    ("retire",   :factor,     findfirst(==("retire"),   cn_ame)),
    ("age",      :continuous, findfirst(==("age"),      cn_ame)),
    ("hstatusg", :factor,     findfirst(==("hstatusg"), cn_ame)),
    ("hhincome", :continuous, findfirst(==("hhincome"), cn_ame)),
    ("educyear", :continuous, findfirst(==("educyear"), cn_ame)),
    ("married",  :factor,     findfirst(==("married"),  cn_ame)),
    ("hisp",     :factor,     findfirst(==("hisp"),     cn_ame)),
]

margins_dydx_ame_binary_stata(β_ame, V_ame, X_ame;
    specs     = specs_ame,
    link      = :logit,
    depvar    = "ins",
    vce_label = "OIM");
```

### 14.7.4 The prchange equivalent

```{julia}
#=
* Computing change in probability after logit (prchange is deprecated, use margins)
quietly logit ins retire $xlist
margins, dydx(hhincome) atmeans

quietly logit ins retire $xlist
estat classification
=#

# Two Stata commands rolled into one chunk — fit logit once, then run both:
#   (1) `margins, dydx(hhincome) atmeans`
#       → inline `margins_dydx_atmeans_binary_stata` on hhincome
#   (2) `estat classification`
#       → `stata_estat_classification` printing the full block
fml_pc = GLM.@formula(ins ~ retire + age + hstatusg + hhincome +
                             educyear + married + hisp)
pc_fit = stata_logit(df14, fml_pc; quiet = true)

x̄_pc   = vec(Statistics.mean(pc_fit.X_glm, dims = 1))
idx_hh = findfirst(==("hhincome"), pc_fit.coefnames_glm)
margins_dydx_atmeans_binary_stata(pc_fit.β_glm, pc_fit.V_glm, x̄_pc, idx_hh;
    link      = :logit,
    var_name  = "hhincome",
    depvar    = "ins",
    vce_label = "OIM",
    n_obs     = pc_fit.n)

stata_estat_classification(pc_fit.model, df14; depvar = "ins");
```

## 14.8 Endogenous regressors

### 14.8.1 Example

```{julia}
#=
use musr/mus14data.dta, clear

* Endogenous probit using inconsistent probit MLE
generate linc = log(hhincome)
global xlist2 female age age2 educyear married hisp white chronic adl hstatusg
probit ins linc $xlist2, vce(robust) nolog
=#

# `df14` is already loaded with `linc = log(hhincome)` (§14.4.1) and
# `age2 = age·age` (§14.5.1), so the global `xlist2` regressors are all
# present. `stata_probit` fits the inconsistent probit MLE treating `linc` as
# exogenous, with robust SEs.
stata_probit(df14,
    GLM.@formula(ins ~ linc + female + age + age2 + educyear + married +
                        hisp + white + chronic + adl + hstatusg);
    vce = :robust);
```

### 14.8.2 Model assumptions

### 14.8.3 Structural-model approach

#### IV probit (control function approach)

#### Maximum likelihood estimates

```{julia}
#=
* Endogenous probit using ivprobit ML estimator
global ivlist2 retire sretire
ivprobit ins $xlist2 (linc = $ivlist2), vce(robust) nolog
=#

# `stata_ivprobit` is the joint-MLE IV probit:
#   y2 = 1{x'β + γ·y1 + u > 0},  y1 = z'π + δ'IV + v,
#   (u, v) ~ N(0, [[1, ρσ_v]; [ρσ_v, σ_v²]])
# estimated jointly in (β, γ, π, δ, lnσ_v, atanh ρ) by LBFGS with an explicit
# ForwardDiff gradient and warm-started from a probit + OLS first-stage.
# `vce=:robust` uses the sandwich SE. Output mirrors Stata's `Probit model
# with endogenous regressors` block including the auxiliary
# `corr(e.linc, e.ins) / sd(e.linc)` rows, the Wald test of exogeneity, and
# the trailing Endogenous/Exogenous variable lists.
stata_ivprobit(df14;
    depvar      = :ins,
    exog_vars   = [:female, :age, :age2, :educyear, :married, :hisp,
                   :white, :chronic, :adl, :hstatusg],
    endog       = :linc,
    instruments = [:retire, :sretire],
    vce         = :robust);
```

#### Two-step sequential estimates

```{julia}
#=
* Endogenous probit using ivprobit 2-step estimator
ivprobit ins $xlist2 (linc = $ivlist2), twostep first
=#

# `stata_ivprobit_twostep` is Newey's (1987) two-step / control-function IV
# probit:
#   1. First stage: OLS of linc on (exog + instruments) — printed as a
#      standard `regress` block.
#   2. Compute v̂ = first-stage residuals.
#   3. Second stage: probit of ins on (exog + linc + v̂); the slope
#      coefficients on (exog + linc) are the IV-probit estimates.
# Wald test of exogeneity: (z on v̂)² ~ χ²(1). Point estimates and the Wald
# statistic match Stata exactly; the second-stage SEs are the augmented-probit
# OIM SEs (Newey's exact two-step correction is not applied — small
# differences from Stata's reported SEs).
stata_ivprobit_twostep(df14;
    depvar      = :ins,
    exog_vars   = [:female, :age, :age2, :educyear, :married, :hisp,
                   :white, :chronic, :adl, :hstatusg],
    endog       = :linc,
    instruments = [:retire, :sretire]);
```

### 14.8.4 IVs approach

```{julia}
#=
* Endogenous probit using ivregress to get 2SLS estimator
ivregress 2sls ins $xlist2 (linc = $ivlist2), vce(robust) noheader
estat overid
=#

# Two StatEcon helpers:
#   1. `stata_ivregress_2sls(df, y, endog => iv, exog; robust, first)` fits
#      2SLS via FixedEffectModels.reg and prints the Stata-format table with
#      the Endogenous/Exogenous footer. `first = false` suppresses the
#      first-stage regressions.
#   2. `stata_estat_overid_2sls` computes the Sargan-style score test of
#      overidentifying restrictions via the auxiliary regression
#      `û_2sls ~ exog + instruments`, statistic = n·R² ~ χ²(L−K).
stata_ivregress_2sls(df14, :ins,
    :linc => [:retire, :sretire],
    [:female, :age, :age2, :educyear, :married, :hisp,
     :white, :chronic, :adl, :hstatusg];
    robust = true, first = false)

stata_estat_overid_2sls(df14;
    depvar      = :ins,
    exog_vars   = [:female, :age, :age2, :educyear, :married, :hisp,
                   :white, :chronic, :adl, :hstatusg],
    endog       = :linc,
    instruments = [:retire, :sretire]);
```

```{julia}
#=
* 2SLS estimation
ivregress 2sls ins $xlist (retire= female sretire seprhi adl), robust
estimates store TSLS_IV4
=#

# Same `stata_ivregress_2sls` helper as the previous chunk, but with `retire`
# as the endogenous variable, the original `xlist` (no `linc`/`female`) as
# exogenous, and the four-instrument set {female, sretire, seprhi, adl}. The
# first-stage regressions are shown here (`first = true`, the default).
stata_ivregress_2sls(df14, :ins,
    :retire => [:female, :sretire, :seprhi, :adl],
    [:age, :hstatusg, :hhincome, :educyear, :married, :hisp];
    robust = true);
```

## 14.9 Grouped data

### 14.9.1 Estimation with aggregate data

### 14.9.2 Grouped-data application

```{julia}
#=
* Using musr/mus14data.dta to generate grouped data
sort age
collapse av_ret=retire av_hhinc=hhincome av_educyear=educyear         ///
        av_mar=married av_adl=adl av_hisp=hisp av_hstatusg=hstatusg   ///
        av_ins=ins, by(age)
generate logins = log(av_ins/(1-av_ins))
save musr/mus14gdata.dta, replace
=#

# Stata's `collapse <vars> by(age)` aggregates each variable to its group
# mean. We emulate it with `combine(groupby(df14, :age), …)`, then build
# `logins = log(av_ins/(1-av_ins))` (the logit transform of the group-mean
# Pr(ins)) and save to mus14gdata.csv (Julia's native equivalent of Stata's
# .dta save).
df14g = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df14, :age),
    :retire   => Statistics.mean => :av_ret,
    :hhincome => Statistics.mean => :av_hhinc,
    :educyear => Statistics.mean => :av_educyear,
    :married  => Statistics.mean => :av_mar,
    :adl      => Statistics.mean => :av_adl,
    :hisp     => Statistics.mean => :av_hisp,
    :hstatusg => Statistics.mean => :av_hstatusg,
    :ins      => Statistics.mean => :av_ins)
DataFrames.sort!(df14g, :age)
df14g.logins = [(0 < p < 1) ? log(p / (1 - p)) : missing for p in df14g.av_ins]

CSV.write("mus14gdata.csv", df14g)
Printf.@printf("Grouped data: %d age cells, columns = %s\n",
        DataFrames.nrow(df14g),
        join(string.(DataFrames.names(df14g)), ", "));
```

```{julia}
#=
*Summarize grouped data
summarize logins  $xlistg
=#

# Stata global `$xlistg` is the grouped version of `$xlist`:
#   av_ret av_hhinc av_educyear av_mar av_adl av_hisp av_hstatusg
# Use `stata_summarize` on `df14g` (built in the previous chunk) for the
# standard Stata-style summary block.
stata_summarize(df14g, [:logins, :av_ret, :av_hhinc, :av_educyear,
                        :av_mar, :av_adl, :av_hisp, :av_hstatusg]);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Regressions with grouped data
regress logins av_ret av_hstatusg av_hhinc av_educyear av_mar av_hisp, vce(robust)
=#

# Drop the cells with missing `logins` (boundary av_ins ∈ {0, 1}) so OLS only
# sees finite values, then `stata_regress` prints the standard Stata `regress`
# block with `vce(robust)`.
df14g_reg = DataFrames.dropmissing(df14g, :logins)
stata_regress(df14g_reg, :logins,
              [:av_ret, :av_hstatusg, :av_hhinc, :av_educyear,
               :av_mar, :av_hisp];
              vcov = FixedEffectModels.Vcov.robust());
```

## 14.10 Stata resources

## 14.11 Exercises
